content:
We propose a new simple network architecture, the Transformer, based solely on attention mechanisms, dispensing with recurrence and convolutions entirely.

An attention function can be described as mapping a query and a set of key-value pairs to an output.
The output is computed as a weighted sum of the values.
```python
# Self Attention implementation block
def self_attention(query, key, value):
 scores = query @ key.T / sqrt(d_k)
 weights = softmax(scores)
 return weights @ value
```
---
*Created by Vaswani et al.*
title: 1. Abstract
section_path: Transformer Architecture and Attention Mechanism > 1. Abstract
author: Antigravity

content:
Vector Databases and HNSW Indexes

Approximate Nearest Neighbor (ANN) search algorithms are critical for high-dimensional vector search.

HNSW (Hierarchical Navigable Small World) structures data in multi-layered graphs. The parameters M (max edges per node) and ef_construct (search range during build) control the precision vs build speed trade-off.
title: Optimizing Vector Databases using HNSW
author: Antigravity

content:
Reciprocal Rank Fusion (RRF) algorithm definition:
RRF is a simple and effective ranking algorithm for combining search results from multiple information retrieval strategies.
RRF works by calculating a score based on the reciprocal of the rank of a document in each strategy.
RRF score = sum( 1 / (60 + rank_i) ) where 60 is a smoothing constant.
It is highly robust because it does not depend on the absolute score values from dense and sparse retrievers.
author: Antigravity
